text: 43.9g of fat. omega3 is where things start to fall off. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
30.2g of carbs. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.
18.2g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
everything else is kind of whatever.
magnesium at 73% daily value, zinc at 39% daily value, iron at 37% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

on the plus side: holds up as a better snack default than most packaged options. that real-world swap value counts. fat and texture help satiety. they can hold up better than many snack foods. portable, practical snack food. nuts score real points in life because they are easy to use.

on the weaker side: portion control can get slippery fast. dense foods punish casual handful math. cost or allergy issues can limit use. practical downsides still count. good versions can get expensive. that affects how realistic frequent use is.
C tier. Best only in small amounts when the calories stay under control.
